data.title: Blockchain Capital is suspected of transferring 216,000 AAVE tokens to Coinbase Prime, worth approximately $24.8 million.
data.description: According to crypto analyst EmberCN, investment firm Blockchain Capital has transferred all of its 216,000 AAVE tokens to Coinbase Prime, worth approximately $24.8 million. The firm had previously purchased approximately 233,000 AAVE tokens between October 2020 and March 2023, at an average price of around $94, and had long been one of the major institutional holders of AAVE. Previously, near the AAVE price peak in December 2024, Blockchain Capital sold 16,900 AAVE tokens through FalconX, worth approximately $5.04 million. With this transfer, its early holdings have essentially been divested.

data.title: Energy Supply Crunch Threatens Global Food Crop Production
data.description: Food crops are increasingly at risk due to the energy supply crunch exacerbated by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that farmers across Asia and Europe are experiencing a shortage of fuel necessary for operating vital agricultural machinery. This scarcity poses a significant threat to food production, as the availability of energy resources is crucial for the cultivation and harvesting of crops. The situation underscores the interconnectedness of global energy markets and agricultural output, with potential implications for food security worldwide.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation released the "EF Mandate," emphasizing the principles of self-sovereignty and decentralization.
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation (ETH) released the "EF Mandate," serving as its "charter, manifesto, and action guide," clarifying its mission, decision-making principles, and role within the Ethereum ecosystem. The document states that Ethereum's core goal is to guarantee user self-sovereignty, meaning users have ultimate control over their identity, assets, and actions. The Foundation emphasizes that Ethereum's development must always adhere to four core attributes: censorship resistance, open source and freedom, privacy, and security (collectively known as CROPS), stating that these principles should not be sacrificed for efficiency or convenience. It also places Ethereum within a broader open technology ecosystem, referring to it as part of the "Infinite Garden," an open digital infrastructure network jointly built by developers, the community, and institutions. The ETH Foundation states that its role is that of a "guardian" of the project, not an owner or ruler, and its responsibility is to maintain the network's long-term vision and open values. The Foundation also emphasizes that as the ecosystem matures, its influence should gradually decrease, with more responsibilities being assumed by the broader community and developer ecosystem.

data.title: CFTC Chairman: The CFTC is setting standards and recruiting talent for cryptocurrencies, AI, and prediction markets.
data.description: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig stated in a post on the X platform that the CFTC is developing applicable rules and standards for major innovations in the golden age of US financial markets, including cryptocurrencies, AI, and prediction markets. It is seeking top talent in the technology and marketing fields to help the agency navigate into the future.

data.title: U.S. Interior Secretary Discusses Oil Futures Market Strategy
data.description: U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum has revealed that American officials have considered using the oil futures market as a strategy to curb the surge in crude oil prices. According to Jin10, this discussion highlights the ongoing efforts by the U.S. government to manage energy costs and stabilize the market amid fluctuating global oil prices. The strategy aims to address the economic impact of rising oil prices on both consumers and industries. This approach reflects the broader governmental measures being evaluated to ensure energy affordability and market stability.

data.title: South Korean Prime Minister Addresses U.S. Trade Concerns
data.description: South Korean Prime Minister has commented on recent statements made by U.S. Trade Representative Greer, indicating that South Korea may not be the target of a Section 301 investigation under U.S. trade law. According to Jin10, the remarks come amid ongoing discussions about international trade relations and potential investigations that could impact South Korea's economic interactions with the United States. The Prime Minister's statement aims to clarify South Korea's position and alleviate concerns regarding potential trade disputes. This development is part of broader efforts to maintain stable trade relations between the two countries.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Commerce Department Withdraws AI Chip Export Regulation
data.description: The U.S. Commerce Department has withdrawn a proposed regulation that aimed to limit the export of artificial intelligence chips globally without prior approval from the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this decision in the context of international trade and technology development. The draft regulation was initially intended to control the distribution of advanced AI technology, reflecting concerns over national security and technological competitiveness. The move to retract the regulation suggests a reconsideration of the balance between innovation and security in the AI sector. This development is likely to impact the global AI chip market, influencing both manufacturers and consumers worldwide.

data.title: Sources: Meta may lay off at least 20% of its staff.
data.description: According to foreign media citing sources, Meta plans a large-scale layoff that could affect 20% or more of its workforce, as Meta seeks to offset its expensive bets on artificial intelligence infrastructure and prepare for improving the efficiency of AI-assisted employees. The sources stated that the date and scale of the layoffs are undetermined. If the layoffs reach 20%, it will be Meta's most significant round of layoffs since its restructuring, dubbed the "Year of Efficiency," in late 2022 and early 2023. (Jinshi)

data.title: FAA Lifts Ground Stop Orders at Washington's Major Airports
data.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) has announced the lifting of ground stop orders at three major airports in the Washington area. According to Jin10, the directive was initially implemented due to unspecified reasons affecting air traffic control operations. The affected airports included Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport, Washington Dulles International Airport, and Baltimore/Washington International Thurgood Marshall Airport. The FAA has not provided further details on the cause of the disruption or the duration of the ground stop. Travelers are advised to check with airlines for updated flight information.

data.title: U.S. Department of Energy Announces First Tender for 86 Million Barrels of Crude Oil
data.description: The U.S. Department of Energy has announced its first tender involving 86 million barrels of crude oil. According to Jin10, this move is part of a broader strategy to manage the nation's energy resources effectively. The tender aims to optimize the strategic petroleum reserve and ensure energy security. This announcement comes amid fluctuating global oil prices and ongoing discussions about energy independence. The Department of Energy's decision reflects its commitment to maintaining a stable energy supply while addressing market dynamics.

data.title: Military Strategy and Its Impact on Conflict Resolution
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. The recent military actions, including the elimination of leadership and the bombing of 5,000 locations, have raised questions about the long-term strategy and its effectiveness in conflict resolution. Analysts are debating whether such aggressive tactics will lead to sustainable peace or further instability in the region. The focus remains on understanding the implications of these actions and their potential to influence future geopolitical dynamics.

data.title: U.S. Air Force Tankers Damaged in Attack at Saudi Air Base
data.description: Five U.S. Air Force refueling aircraft were reportedly attacked and damaged at the Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia. According to Jin10, the incident occurred on the tarmac, raising concerns about the security of military assets in the region. The attack highlights ongoing geopolitical tensions and the vulnerability of strategic locations in the Middle East. Further details about the perpetrators or the extent of the damage have not been disclosed. The situation underscores the need for heightened security measures and diplomatic efforts to address regional instability.

data.title: STOCKS | New Generation of Investors Embrace 24/7 Trading Opportunities
data.description: A new wave of investors is increasingly turning to markets that operate around the clock, seeking to leverage the advantages of continuous trading. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting how these investors are moving away from traditional market hours to explore opportunities in sectors that offer 24/7 access. This shift is driven by the desire for greater flexibility and the ability to respond to global events in real-time, without being constrained by conventional trading schedules. As technology continues to evolve, the appeal of non-stop trading platforms is expected to grow, attracting more participants who value the ability to trade at any time.

data.title: Hong Kong Bistro Showcases East-Meets-West Culinary Style
data.description: Vicky Lau has opened a new bistro within Hong Kong's Kimpton, showcasing her distinctive East-meets-West culinary approach. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the Yunnan-inspired menu that surprises diners with its innovative dishes. Lau's latest venture continues to reflect her unique style, blending diverse culinary traditions to create a memorable dining experience.

data.title: Iran's Strategic Moves in Global Oil Market Amid Tensions
data.description:
The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. The ongoing geopolitical tensions have led to significant strategic maneuvers by Iran in the global oil market. The U.S. has made its intentions clear regarding regime change in Iran, actively targeting its leadership. In response, Iran appears to be adopting a strategy aimed at impacting the global economy by influencing oil prices.

Iran's approach seems to be driven by the belief that it cannot match the U.S. in a direct conflict. Instead, it is focusing on leveraging its position in the oil market to exert pressure. By potentially pushing oil prices to destabilizing levels, Iran aims to challenge the global economic balance.

This strategy involves a delicate balance, as Iran must consider how far it can push oil prices while maintaining the flow of its energy assets. The situation underscores the complex interplay between geopolitical strategies and economic impacts in the region.

data.title: Crypto industry lobbying groups have spent approximately $271 million to influence the 2026 US election.
data.description: Data shows that crypto lobbying groups have already spent approximately $271 million to influence the 2026 US election, primarily on political activities such as advertising. The super PAC FairShake is one of the main sources of funding, with donors including Coinbase, Ripple, Uniswap Labs, and venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz. The data also shows that approximately 40% of the funds have gone to Republican candidates, about 3% to Democratic candidates, and the remainder to independents. Industry insiders say that as the 2026 US midterm elections approach, crypto lobbying activity is expected to continue to increase. (DL News)

data.title: Circle launches open-source AI development tool Circle Skills, supporting integration with stablecoins such as USDC and EURC.
data.description: Circle has released open-source AI development tools called Circle Skills for building applications based on USDC, EURC, Arc, and the Circle developer platform. Developers and AI agents can invoke this component through Cursor, Claude Code, Codex, and other Agent tools that support Skills to generate integrated solutions related to stablecoin payments, cross-chain transfers, wallet operations, and smart contract logic.

data.title: Bitcoin's Price Movement Mirrors Early Ukraine War Reaction Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Bitcoin's price behavior in response to the ongoing Iran conflict is showing similarities to its reaction during the initial month of the Ukraine war. According to NS3.AI, after an initial sell-off, Bitcoin's price is returning to the $70,000–$73,000 range. The analysis highlights comparable swings in the Relative Strength Index (RSI) and Chaikin Money Flow (CMF), suggesting a pattern of volatile consolidation rather than a prolonged downturn.

data.title: U.S. Seeks Extradition of Maduro Ally from Venezuela
data.description: The Trump administration is collaborating with Venezuela's interim government to extradite a significant ally of former leader Nicolás Maduro to the United States for prosecution. Bloomberg posted on X, citing sources familiar with the matter, that the charges could involve money laundering. This move is part of ongoing efforts to address corruption and financial crimes linked to the previous Venezuelan regime. The extradition process underscores the U.S. commitment to supporting Venezuela's interim government in its transition and legal proceedings against former officials.

data.title: Judge to Decide on Dismissal of $25 Million MEV Bot Scheme Charges
data.description: James Peraire-Bueno, during a Friday hearing in New York, argued for the acquittal of his alleged $25 million MEV bot scheme instead of facing a retrial. According to NS3.AI, Judge Jessica Clarke stated she would make a decision on the request to dismiss the charges before any new trial proceedings, which are not expected to commence before November.
